x-enumeration-literals.name: Bullet / Lump-sum Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Entire principal is paid at once on maturity

Comment:
Very common for bonds and term deposits

x-enumeration-literals.name: Installment / Amortized Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Principal is repaid in parts over time

Comment:
Common in loans, some structured notes

x-enumeration-literals.name: Partial Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Only part of the outstanding amount is redeemed

Comment:
Can be scheduled or ad-hoc

x-enumeration-literals.name: Early / Call Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redemption before maturity, initiated by the bank (issuer)

Comment:
Subject to call conditions and notice period

x-enumeration-literals.name: Put Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Early redemption initiated by the holder

Comment:
Bank must repay if put option is exercised

x-enumeration-literals.name: Mandatory Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redemption occurs automatically on a specified date or event

Comment:
No option involved

x-enumeration-literals.name: Event-based Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Triggered by a specific event

Comment:
Examples: regulatory change, default, merger

x-enumeration-literals.name: Rolling / Periodic Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description: Redeemed at regular intervals (monthly, quarterly, etc.)
